titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the David Goldblatt Photographs and Papers
titlestmt.author: by Jessica Tai, Jim Fisher, and Mike Rush

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This collection contains 9 published digital objects. And those objects are comprised of 70 digital files.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday April 3, 2025 at 23:02

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
This collection contains photographic prints, contact sheets, negatives, slides, research material, printed material, correspondence, and other papers by or relating to South African photographer David Goldblatt. The work represented in the collection primarily consists of Goldblatt's personal photographic work, but also includes Goldblatt's work as a professional photographer for magazines, corporations, advertising agencies, and other institutions. Major projects and exhibitions represented in the collection include Some Afrikaners Photographed,
The Transported of Kwandebele: A South African Odyssey , David Goldblatt: Structures of Dominion and Democracy , In Boksburg , and On the Mines . Professional work includes projects completed for Leadership Magazine , Fair Lady , Observer Magazine , The New York Times , and Standard Bank.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged into six series: Series I: Personal work, circa 1940s-2016. Series II: Corporate work, circa 1960-2018. Series III: Magazine work, circa 1948-2011. Series IV: Correspondence, 1964-2017. Series V: Printed material, 1986-2018. Series VI: Professional papers, 1960-2018.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The David Goldblatt Photographs and Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: David Goldblatt Photographs and Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection or revisions in arrangement and description.

bioghist.head: David Goldblatt
bioghist.p: David Goldblatt (1930 - 2018) was a South African photographer who most notably documented the people, landscapes, and structures of South Africa under apartheid, as well as chronicling its dismantling and legacy after the first free elections in 1994. Goldblatt was born in Randfontein, South Africa and graduated from the University of the Witwatersrand with a degree in commerce. Following the death of his father in 1962, he sold the family business and devoted himself full-time to photography. Goldblatt's personal work focused on understanding South African's relationships to each other and their physical environment during and after apartheid. His major projects include On the Mines , 1973, Some Afrikaners Photographed , 1975, In Boksburg , 1982, and his series, Structures , which he worked on from 1948 to 2018.

In 1989, Goldblatt founded the Market Photography Workshop in Johannesburg. In 1998 he was the first South African to have a solo exhibition at the Museum of Modern Art in New York. Goldblatt died in 2018 in Johannesburg at the age of 87.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: David Goldblatt's library of photographic books, including a complete set of his publications, is in the collection of the Wits Arts Museum in Johannesburg, South Africa.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from David Goldblatt on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2019.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains 35mm, 120 and 4x5 inch black and white and color negatives, contact sheets, and proof prints of Goldblatt's personal work. Work from projects include Barcelona , Boksburg , Carlton Centre , Ex- offenders , Fietas , Hillbrow , Intersections , Johannesburg , On the Mines , Particulars , Shaft Sinker , Some Afrikaners , Soweto , Structures and Land , and Transkei .
David Goldblatt worked in film until 1999, and digitally thereafter. Digital personal work is represented in the archive among the proof prints.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged into three subseries: Negatives, circa 1940s-1999; Contact sheets, circa 1948-2010; and Proof prints, circa 1950-2016.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Personal work may also be found in the Corporate work and Magazine work series.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains negatives from Goldblatt's personal photographic work.

For each negative format, Goldblatt assigned a prefix number, followed by a forward slash, or underscore, and sequential number. Some of these numbers can be referenced in Goldblatt's negative index notebooks, which are located in Boxes 170 and 171.

Negative formats include 35 mm, indicated by the prefix 3, and followed by a sequential number. After the first 10,000 frames, a sequential prefix letter was given after every 10,000 frames. The series also includes 120 negatives, represented by the prefix 2, and followed by a sequential number.

The series also contains 4 x 5 inch negatives and are represented by the prefix 4, followed by a sequential number. Some 4 x 5 inch negatives include accompanying index cards that record technical and other informational details of the negative. Not all negatives are in sequential order, and some gaps appear throughout the series.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Material is arranged by negative format, and further arranged by Goldblatt's original organizational system for negatives. Titled projects are located at the end of the series.

did.unittitle: 120 negatives GBW_0001/4 to GBW_0029/32 and contact sheets

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains discontinuous negatives with a large date range.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains unnumbered negatives not matched to index cards.

did.unittitle: 4x5 inch negative and contact sheet: "journalists on trial during state of emergency"
"Barlow Rand: Black Education, Fair Lady": prints, negatives, contact sheets, and notes
Cape Dutch Homesteads and Struik negatives
"Haggie and Manim": negatives and contact sheets
"Post Office M.D., Frank Touwen Pro, Ben Rootman": photographs, negatives, and slides
10x12 inch negatives: "personal work, print process images"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains contact sheets made from 35mm, 120mm, and 4 x 5 inch negatives of series from Goldblatt's personal work. Projects include Barcelona , Boksburg , Carlton Centre , Hillbrow , Intersections , Johannesburg , Mining men and mines , Shaft Sinker , Soweto , Transkei , and others.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Material in this series is arranged according to Goldblatt's original organizational system for negative numbers. For more information, consult the Negatives subseries in the Personal Work series.
Goldblatt kept his contacts in a cabinet referred to as the "Chinese chest." Contacts that were removed from the Chinese chest by Goldblatt have not been interfiled with the main sequence.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Additional contact sheets for personal work can be found interfiled within the proof prints subseries.

did.unittitle: Contact sheets A16-19 to A9996-10001, B1-6
Contact sheets B5004-09 to B9996-C2, C3-8 to C15-17
Contact sheets 2/30044-46 to 2/31939-41, 2/40015-17 to 2/40073-75, 1 color contact

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: includes one color contact

did.unittitle: Binder with contact sheets, prints, and notes
Contacts removed from Chinese chest by David Goldblatt

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: David Goldblatt kept proof prints in photographic print boxes or other encolosures according to negative number, project, or subject. Beinecke Library staff maintained Goldblatt's groupings as found at the time of receipt. Proof prints identified by negative number are listed first, followed by prints grouped by project or subject, in roughly the order in which they were found upon receipt.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Proof prints for Goldblatt's personal work may also be found in the Corporate work or Magazine work series.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: The materials listed within this subseries were identified as personal work by the Goldblatt family prior to transfer to the Beinecke Library. Beinecke Library staff made minimal changes to the arrangement of the materials in the process of rehousing materials into archival containers.

Quotation marks indicate that Beinecke Library staff transcribed the description of proof prints from Goldblatt's label. When labels were difficult to read library staff photocopied the label and added it the file.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The proof prints subseries primarily consists of black and white or color working prints of photographs David Goldblatt identified as personal work. The proof prints include work from projects including Barcelona , Boksburg , Fietas , Hillbrow , Intersections , Johannesburg , On the Mines , Shaft Sinker , Some Afrikaners , Soweto , Structures and Land , Transkei , and others. Also includes some contacts.

did.unittitle: "Early rough prints-Cape town, Father Martin, Durban rickshaws and stevedores, Mashu"
"Portraits-Zackie Achmat, Uys Krige, Harry Oppenheimer, and others"
"Portraits- Sam and Alida Haskins, Helen Josephs, and others"
"Portraits-Johan van Heerden, Alan Paton, and others"
"Portraits-Raymond Mhlaba, Govan Mbeki, and unknown activist"
"Portraits-Cosmos Desmond, Beyers Nawde, Dr Wendy Orr, Zwelakhe Sisulu, and Helen Suzman

"Portraits-Johan Rupert, Craig Williamson, Judge Heath, and Hugh Masakela
"Portraits- Gerrit Viljoen, Bruce Fordyce, Lionel Abrams, and others"
"Portraits-Hugh Murray?, Mrs. Turner/mother of Rick Turner, and Ken Owen
"Portraits-Jennifer Ferguson, Roelf Meyer, and Jeremy Cronin"
"Portraits-Marcel Golding + Jonny Copeland, Gavin Reilly, Chris Lessing, and Richard Richard Maponya"
"For Yale Nadine Gordimer archive and prints for authentication"
Nelson Mandela-slides, negatives and contact sheets

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials also include slides of Mandela with Murray Crawford and others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Some prints have handwritten descriptions.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Prints are copies from multiple projects, in both color and black and white.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Material includes corporate work and portraits.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Material includes corporate work and portraits.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Material includes corporate work and portraits.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: David Goldblatt kept his corporate work in envelopes or other enclosures, grouped by commission. The job files maintain that arrangement, roughly in the order in which they were found at the time of receipt.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Corporate work may also be found in the Personal work and Magazine work series.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: The materials listed within this series were identified as corporate work by the Goldblatt family prior to transfer to the Beinecke Library. Beinecke Library staff made minimal changes to the arrangement of the materials in the process of rehousing materials into archival containers.
Quotation marks indicate that Beinecke Library staff transcribed a job file title from Goldblatt's label. When labels were difficult to read library staff photocopied the label and added it the file.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Corporate work files include a variety of materials, including negatives, prints, contacts, correspondence, and other papers, documenting Goldblatt's projects for corporate clients. The files document Goldblatt's work for a range of banks, mining companies, manufacturers, hotels, and other organizations, including Standard Bank, Barclays Bank, De Beers, Barlow Rand, Volkskas Limited Bank, Royal Hotel, Marine Hotel, and Stellenbosch Family Winery.

did.unittitle: Standard Bank/Lintas advertising brief, prints, negatives
Standard Bank "Aerobics at gym/ ballroom dancing "
"The perm advertising campaign, Soccer and Leach (character)"
"South African Airways advertising campaign/ Lindsay Smithers"
"BMW Advert / Philliips Bunton Mundel and Blake"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes negatives and contacts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes negatives and contacts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contacts of "Oppenheimer Estate?", Durban dock workers, and others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs include Leyland, VW, Castle Beer and others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials include receipts and documentation.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes color negatives and contact sheets of portraits of Sydney Press at his home office in Imanda House, Johannesburg, as well as interior shots of the office. Also includes color transparencies of portraits of another businessman in a factory.

did.unittitle: Bannie Britz and Michael Scholes-correspondence and receipts
"EOS and oversize Boksburg", prints and and printed materials
"Political portraits, Royal Hotel, Press Farm", prints

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes portraits of Sydney Arnold Press in his home library and garden at Inanda House (architect: Sir Herbert Baker) in Johannesburg.

did.unittitle: "Coromandel/Edgardale/Edgars Various and Sydney Press", contacts, prints, negatives and printed materials

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Edgardale was the headquarters for Edgars Stores. The architect was Marco Zanuso, and it was completed in 1978.
Press Farm is properly known as Coromandel Farm.
David Goldblatt collaborated with Margaret Courtney-Clarke to photograph the interiors of Coromandel House.

did.unittitle: "Truth and Reconciliation hearings", prints, negatives and printed materials
"Eddie Rubenstein of Glen Anil", prints and negatives
"Political Development, Port Elizabeth and Uttenhage Township", negatives and printed materials

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes exterior and interior photographs of Coromandel House. Goldblatt collaborated with Margaret Courtney-Clarke on the interior photographs of Coromandel House.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subseries: Job files, circa 1960-2011, and Transparencies, circa 1970s-1980s.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Magazine work series consists of job files and transparencies documenting David Goldblatt's work for magazines and other periodicals.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Magazine work may also be found in the Personal work and Corporate work series.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: The materials listed within this series were identified as magazine work by the Goldblatt family prior to transfer to the Beinecke Library. Beinecke Library staff made minimal changes to the arrangement of the materials in the process of rehousing materials into archival containers.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: David Goldblatt kept his magazine work in envelopes or other enclosures, grouped by commission. The job files maintain that arrangement, roughly in the order in which they were found at the time of receipt.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Magazine work job files include a variety of materials, including negatives, prints, contacts, correspondence, and other papers, documenting Goldblatt's projects for magazines and periodicals. The files document Goldblatt's work for a range of publications, including Leadership , Optima , National Geographic , The New York Times , The Sunday Times , Fortune Magazine , Observer Magazine , The Los Angeles Times , Fair Lady Magazine , and others.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Quotation marks indicate that Beinecke Library staff transcribed a job file title from Goldblatt's label. When labels were difficult to read library staff photocopied the label and added it the file.

"Mostly Fairlady" a variety of subjects. Prints

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Transparencies are arranged in the order in which they were found at the time of receipt.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Transparencies consist of slides and other transparencies (both black and white and color), along with some negatives and notes, related to magazine work by David Goldblatt. Subjects include Johannesburg, Soweto, mines, Namibia, Bophutaswana, Transkei, Barlow Rand, Lesotho, Sterns, Standard Bank, Boksburg, Oberammergau, East London, Swaziland, and Nice (France).
The transparencies include work for Leadership , National Geographic , Observer Magazine , and other publications. The photographs of Nice relate to a collaboration with Nadine Gordimer.
Transparencies may also include personal or corporate work.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Titles in quotes are transcribed from David Goldblatt's labels.
Beinecke Library staff rehoused the slides from slide boxes into archival slide sheets.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series primarily contains professional correspondence in addition to notes, project files, and photographic prints. Correspondence relates to Goldblatt's South African as well as International museum and gallery exhibitions, workshops, book projects, and conferences.

did.unittitle: Correspondence: "On the Mines" and "Some Afrikaners Photographed"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence from David Goldblatt, Nadine Gordimer, Peter Struik, Gerrit Struik, Rene Gordon, and others.

did.unittitle: Correspondence and exhibition notes: Australian Centre for Photography, Art Gallery of Western Australia and Wittenoom exhibition
Correspondence and notes: Cape Dutch Homes and Struik

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence from David Goldblatt, Nadine Gordimer, Ezra Stoller, and others.

did.unittitle: Correspondence: Ezra Stoller Exhibition and Johannesburg Art Gallery

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence from David Goldblatt, Ezra Stoller, Erica Stoller, and C.E.V. de la Mare.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence from David Goldblatt, Raymond Tucker, and others.

did.unittitle: Correspondence and notes: South African Photographic Gallery
Correspondence and project documents: The Transported of Kwandebele: A South African Odyssey

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence from David Goldblatt, Steve Dietz, Margaret Sartor, Barbara Levine, Alex Harris, and others.

did.unittitle: Correspondence and project files: South Africa: The Structure of Things Then

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence from David Goldblatt, Mark Holborn, Roger Conover, Chris Killip, John Coetzee, Alex Harris, Helen Laurenson, Iris Tillman Hill, and others.

did.unittitle: Correspondence and prints: Harvard University Department of Visual and Environmental Studies

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence from David Goldblatt, Chris Killip, Paul Bell, Steven Goldblatt, and others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence from David Goldblatt, Alex Harris, Marilyn Martin, Sabine Panzram, and others.

did.unittitle: Correspondence: MOMA, Joyce Ozynsky Phaidon, SFMOMA, San Francisco Art Institute
Correspondence: fan mail and letters of appreciation
Correspondence and report: Market Theatre Photo Workshop

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondents include Peter Appel, B.B.C., Cabinet des Estampes, Creative Camera, and Daily Dispatch.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondents include Afrapix, Anglo American Property Services Ltd., BBC, Creative Camera, Duke University, and Mark Holborn.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondents include Africana Museum, Eve Arnold, The Bodley Head, Carlton Centre Limited, Camera magazine, Creative Camera, Edgars, The Economist, George Eastman House, McKechnie Brothers South Africa, and Sinar.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondents include Creative Camera, Eaglemoss Publications, Exile Editions, ICA, and MoMA.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondents include Durban Art Gallery, ELC Art and Craft Centre, Thomas Ernst, and National Geographic Magazine.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondents include History Workshop, Hunter College, Impressions Gallery of Photography, Interview, Alfred Knopf, Leadership, McGraw-Hill, MoMA, The Observer, Photographu Unlimited, Reader's Digest, Oxford University Press, and Side Gallery and Cinema.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondents include the Observer, Redfield Workshops, Side Gallery and Cinema, Chris Steele-Perkins, Ezra Stoller, and U.S. News.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondents include Ezra Stoller, Sunday Times Magazine, Tiger Oats Limited, Time Magazine,Thames Television PLC, Unisa Art Gallery, University of Cape Town, University of South Africa, University of the Western Cape, Victoria and Albert Museum, and The Weekly Mail.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondents include Ezra Stoller, Thames and Hudson, University of Natal, University of Capetown, The Viking Press, and You and Your Camera.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains printed material featuring Goldblatt's work, in addition to printed reference material such as photographic equipment and camera manuals.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Material is arranged by subject area.

did.unittitle: Equipment manuals and catalogs: Kodak, FOBA, Kinderman, and Ilford Photographic
Magazine printed work: British Journal of Photography, South African Jewish Report, SA Photo
Magazine printed work: Fotografia Reflex, Katalog, Photo-Eye Booklist, and ZAM
Magazine printed work: New York Times Magazine
Magazine printed work: Photo District News

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Material is arranged alphabetically by folder title.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains exhibition material, notebooks, project files, reviews, writings, research materials, posters, a camera, and other material relating to Goldblatt's professional life.

did.unittitle: Drafts of introduction text for South Africa: The Structure of Things Then
Exhibition promotional material for David Goldblatt
Hasselblad 2000FC camera, lens, and accessories in an aluminum case

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Hasselblad 2000FC camera mounted on a tripod head with a Linhof Technika drop bed and bag bellows with a Plannar 80mm f/2.8 lens housed in a leather sack.
Lenses include a Distagon 40mm f/4 lens, a Distagon 50mm f/4 lens, a Schneider-Kreuznach Symmar 100mm f/5.6 lens, a Sonnar 150mm f/4 lens, and a Sonnar 250mm f/5.6 lens.
Lens accessories include a Rollei-Hellrot filter mount as well as a polarized lens filter and two lens hoods, F50 and F80-250, all manufactured by Rollei .
Camera accessories include folded bellows, which extends to approximately 16mm with two rails, 9 cm and 22 cm, as well as a 6 x 6 cm roll film back and a focus screen.

Photograph prints include seven color Polaroid prints probably related to a fashion designer, approximately 2000 as well as a copy photograph print of "Reciprocity Effect Exposure Time Adjustments Curves" for black-and-white film.

Other material in the case includes two packs of lens cleaning paper manufactured by Kodak, four business cards for David Goldblatt, note with the address of Mary Ann Van Der Heever at Sea Point, Western Cape, South Africa, a 12 cm plastic ruler, a 6 cm film spool, a 35mm film canister with two metal nuts, a tube of Ta Fong rubber cement, and a piece of plastic patch, 4 x 16 cm.

Aluminum case, 26 cm high, 47 cm wide, and 18 cm deep, customized by Goldblatt, with hardware mounted on exterior for straps as well as affixed labels from British Airways and South African Airways; the interior of the case is lined with red felt and include dividers and containers constructed with carpet, plastic, plexiglass, tape, and wood.

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: This item is fragile and contains glass. Handle with care.

did.unittitle: Interview transcript: David Goldblatt and Okwui Enwezor
London Photographers Gallery guest list and guest book
Michael MacGarry research report: Prospect and Refuge in Jo'burg Intersections 1999-2002 by David Goldblatt
Newspaper and magazine stories featuring Goldblatt's work

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notebook containing an index of numbered 4x5 negatives that were shot between 1964 and 1987. Contains negatives numberered from 4/1 to 4/6116-17 and a brief description of the image. Dates are also occassionaly documented.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes negatives organized into categories, including: animals, groups, aschool affairs, shops, indoor portraits, interiors, clouds, ships and boats, industrical, technical and commercial, architectural, landscapes, pictorials, still life, people (candid), non-Europeans documentary, documentary-general, outdoor portraits, sports, theater, sea and water, trees, moonlight and lighting, picture stories, and copy.

did.unittitle: Notes and script for "Naming the Guilty" talk, New York
